title: Fort St. George
tagline: Fort St. George — heritage city of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Fort St. George is a much-loved heritage city in Tamil Nadu, known for forts and architecture.
overview.description: Fort St. George (or historically, White Town) is a fortress at the coastal city of Chennai, India. Founded in 1639, it was the first English (later British) fortress in India. The construction of the fort provided the impetus for further settlements and trading activity, in what was originally an uninhabited land.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Namakkal Kavignar Maligai
topPlaces.description: Namakkal Kavignar Maligai is an 11-storied building at the Fort St. George campus in Chennai, India, serving as the power centre of state secretariat of Tamil Nadu.

topPlaces.name: Madras Legislative Council
topPlaces.description: Tamil Nadu Legislative Council was the upper house of the former bicameral legislature of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It began its existence as Madras Legislative Council, the first provincial legislature for Madras Presidency.

topPlaces.name: Chennai Port Trust Centenary Building
topPlaces.description: Chennai Port Trust Centenary Building is a 12-storied building in Chennai, India, serving as the chief administrative building of the Chennai Port Trust. The Port Met Office of the Regional Meteorological Centre also functions from this building and the cyclone detection radar is situated atop the building contained in a giant football-like mascot structure, which serves as a prominent landmark in the harbour area.

topPlaces.name: Chennai Port
topPlaces.description: Chennai Port, formerly known as Madras Port, is the second largest container port of India, behind Mumbai's Jawaharlal Nehru Port also known as Nhava Sheva. The port is the largest one in the Bay of Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Victory War Memorial
topPlaces.description: Victory War Memorial, formerly called the Cupid's bow, is an obelisk memorial in Chennai, India, originally constructed to commemorate the victory of the Allied Armies during World War I (1914–1918) and later became the victory war memorial for World War II (1939–1945), erected in the memory of those from the Madras presidency who died in the wars. Later additions include inscriptions for the Indo-Pakistani war of 1947–1948 and the Sino-Indian War.

topPlaces.name: Raja Annamalai Chettiar Hall
topPlaces.description: The Raja Annamalai Chettiar Hall is an auditorium situated in Chennai, India. It is considered to be one of the city's oldest venues for Carnatic music concerts apart from the T. T. Krishnamachari Auditorium in Mylapore.

topPlaces.name: Tamil Nadu Public Service Commission
topPlaces.description: The Tamil Nadu Public Service Commission (TNPSC) is a government body of the state of Tamil Nadu, India, responsible for the recruitment of candidates for various state government jobs through competitive examinations. It is the successor of the Madras Service Commission, which came into being under an Act of the Madras Legislature in 1929 and was the first Provincial Public Service Commission in India.

topPlaces.name: Rajaji Salai, Chennai
topPlaces.description: Rajaji Salai, also known as North Beach Road or First Line Beach is an arterial road of Chennai, India. Starting from the junction with Ennore Expressway at the Kasimedu fishing harbour, the road runs through Royapuram and the entire length of Chennai Harbour and after passing through the Central Business District of Georgetown, merges with Mount Road on The Island.

items.note: Namakkal Kavignar Maligai is an 11-storied building at the Fort St. George campus in Chennai, India, serving as the power centre of state secretariat of Tamil Nadu.

Tamil Nadu Legislative Council was the upper house of the former bicameral legislature of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It began its existence as Madras Legislative Council, the first provincial legislature for Madras Presidency.

Chennai Port Trust Centenary Building is a 12-storied building in Chennai, India, serving as the chief administrative building of the Chennai Port Trust. The Port Met Office of the Regional Meteorological Centre also functions from this building and the cyclone detection radar is situated atop the building contained in a giant football-like mascot structure, which serves as a prominent landmark in the harbour area.

Chennai Port, formerly known as Madras Port, is the second largest container port of India, behind Mumbai's Jawaharlal Nehru Port also known as Nhava Sheva. The port is the largest one in the Bay of Bengal.

Victory War Memorial, formerly called the Cupid's bow, is an obelisk memorial in Chennai, India, originally constructed to commemorate the victory of the Allied Armies during World War I (1914–1918) and later became the victory war memorial for World War II (1939–1945), erected in the memory of those from the Madras presidency who died in the wars. Later additions include inscriptions for the Indo-Pakistani war of 1947–1948 and the Sino-Indian War.

The Raja Annamalai Chettiar Hall is an auditorium situated in Chennai, India. It is considered to be one of the city's oldest venues for Carnatic music concerts apart from the T. T. Krishnamachari Auditorium in Mylapore.

itinerary.title: Day 3 — Tamil Nadu Public Service Commission & around

items.note: The Tamil Nadu Public Service Commission (TNPSC) is a government body of the state of Tamil Nadu, India, responsible for the recruitment of candidates for various state government jobs through competitive examinations. It is the successor of the Madras Service Commission, which came into being under an Act of the Madras Legislature in 1929 and was the first Provincial Public Service Commission in India.

Rajaji Salai, also known as North Beach Road or First Line Beach is an arterial road of Chennai, India. Starting from the junction with Ennore Expressway at the Kasimedu fishing harbour, the road runs through Royapuram and the entire length of Chennai Harbour and after passing through the Central Business District of Georgetown, merges with Mount Road on The Island.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Fort St. George?
faq.a: The best time to visit Fort St. George is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Fort St. George?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chennai International Airport (16 km) and the nearest railway station is Chennai Central (1 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Fort St. George?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Fort St. George at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Fort St. George?
faq.a: Stays in Fort St. George start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Fort St. George?
faq.a: The top attractions are Namakkal Kavignar Maligai, Madras Legislative Council, Chennai Port Trust Centenary Building, Chennai Port, Victory War Memorial.
